name: The Rosenwald Sheet — Early Florentine (A-order), c. 1500

sections.What it is: Uncut Florentine tarot sheets, c. 1500, at the NGA (Rosenwald Collection); the early A-order pattern.

description:
Uncut sheets of an early **Florentine (A-order)** tarot, late 15th–early 16th century, in the **National Gallery of Art, Washington** (the Lessing J. Rosenwald Collection). The NGA's open-access policy places the images firmly in the public domain — austere, woodcut, and genuinely early. A southern (A-order) counterpart to the northern C-order Cary Sheet.

ANCESTRAL STATUS: **ancestral** to the standard tarot (the Florentine A-order line).

IMAGE: National Gallery of Art open access (NGA object 41321, Rosenwald Collection), rehosted to R2. SOURCES: NGA collection; Dummett, *The Game of Tarot* (1980).
